# YazSes

> YazSes is an offline, on-device voice agent for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Hold a key, speak a command, release — text is typed, code is committed, files open, timers start, notes are saved. No cloud. No API key. No subscription.

YazSes uses faster-whisper or Moonshine v2 for speech-to-text and llama.cpp or Ollama for local LLM inference. It selects one of 20 built-in OS tools based on what you say.

## Core concepts

- **Hold-to-talk**: Hold the hotkey (Space on Linux, Right Option on macOS, Right Ctrl on Windows) to record. Release to transcribe and act.
- **On-device agent**: A local LLM interprets the transcript and selects the right tool — no network required.
- **20 OS tools**: type_text, git_commit, open_file, goto_symbol, volume_set, note_quick, commit_to_memory, recall, send_message, and more.
- **Dual STT stack**: Moonshine v2 (~9 ms, streaming) for short commands; Whisper-large-v3-turbo for long-form dictation.
- **Personal memory**: Encrypted SQLite vector store — voice-triggered commit, recall, and forget.
- **Zero telemetry**: Nothing leaves the machine unless you explicitly configure an OpenAI-compatible backend.
- **Accessibility**: AT-SPI (Linux) and NVDA (Windows) screen-reader announcements. Works with Talon.

## Quick start

```sh
pipx install yazses                      # or apt/snap/brew/winget
yazses doctor                            # check OS prerequisites
yazses model pull qwen3-7b               # download default LLM (~5 GB)
yazses enroll                            # calibrate mic
yazses start
# Hold Space (Linux) / Right Option (macOS) / Right Ctrl (Windows), speak, release.
```

## Who uses this

Developers, writers, accessibility users, and power users who want hands-free control of their computer without sending audio to a cloud service. Commonly used for: code dictation, voice git commits, voice note-taking, media control, and accessibility.

## Alternatives it replaces

Dragon NaturallySpeaking (offline but Windows-only, expensive), Talon Voice (code-focused, no LLM agent), cloud-based dictation (Google, Apple, Microsoft — sends audio to servers).

## Technical details

- **Languages**: Rust (v1.0, default) + Python (v0.4.x, stable)
- **STT**: whisper.cpp (Whisper-large-v3-turbo) + Moonshine v2
- **LLM**: llama.cpp with GBNF tool-call grammar; Ollama backend optional
- **VAD**: Silero neural VAD + RMS gate fallback
- **IPC**: JSON-RPC 2.0 over Unix socket (Linux/macOS) or named pipe (Windows)
- **Memory**: sqlite-vec encrypted store, AES-256-GCM
- **License**: Apache 2.0
